title: Global Store Architecture
summary: Stress-tested global Fabric store architecture. Verdict: use stable human-readable mount names under stores/, keep store_uuid as immutable identity, split physical stores into personal and shared/org, support personal + N shared read-set, route non-personal writes by semantic_scope, and keep personal scope physically isolated forever.
decisions: A project may read from multiple shared/org stores plus implicit personal.
Non-personal writes should route by semantic_scope, not one global active_write_store.
stores/ should use stable human-readable mount_name directories, not UUID directories as the primary UX path.
Personal-scope knowledge must always physically live in the personal store.
risks: Existing active_write_store single-alias field is too coarse for multi shared-store write routing.
Current stores/<uuid> disk layout conflicts with desired human-facing shape.
The semantic scope 'team' is currently overloaded with the shared store alias.
Migration must handle orphan UUID dirs and store.json/dir-name mismatches.
recommended_next: Create architecture spec for mount_name + scope-aware write_routes.
Supersede or reject pending single-git-kb-all-scopes decision.
Plan migration from stores/<uuid> to stores/<mount_name> with UUID validation.
Upgrade fabric info to explain read-set and write target per scope.
